summary: Every product row contains three nullable prices that can independently create output rows, so an exact unpivot requires a linear source scan; three fixed UNION ALL branches match that lower bound.

optimality.argument: Changing any uninspected product row from all NULL prices to one non-NULL store price changes the required output. An exact query must therefore inspect all R rows and their fixed three price fields. The three UNION ALL branches each perform one filtered scan, for 3R constant-time field checks and O(R) logical time.

replacement_checks.evidence: Any source row can independently contribute zero through three result rows, so skipping it cannot distinguish inputs with different required outputs.
Each of the three fixed store branches scans Products once, tests one field for NULL, and emits at most one row per source product; UNION ALL performs no duplicate elimination.

Authored fixtures cover all-null products, every individual store branch, all stores present, repeated equal prices, zero and negative non-null values, sparse products, and nonconsecutive identifiers.
